title: The Birth of Orpheus
description: Orpheus, son of the muse Calliope, is born with a gift for music that moves the very world around him.
story: From his first breath, Orpheus hummed melodies that made the river slow and the birds circle overhead. His mother placed a lyre in his hands before he could walk. The gods themselves leaned in to listen.

title: The Lyre of Apollo
description: Apollo gifts Orpheus a golden lyre, and his music becomes a force that bends nature itself.
story: With Apollo's lyre, Orpheus could coax trees to uproot and follow him down mountain paths. Rivers reversed their courses at a single chord. Even the stones wept when he played in a minor key.

title: The World Meets Eurydice
description: Orpheus encounters Eurydice in a sunlit meadow and falls instantly, irreversibly in love.
story: She was laughing at something when he first saw her, her dark hair catching the afternoon light. He forgot every melody he had ever known and had to invent a new one just to speak to her. She listened, then laughed again — this time at him, warmly.

title: A Wedding in Spring
description: Orpheus and Eurydice marry beneath an open sky, surrounded by music and the blessing of the gods.
story: The wedding feast lasted three days, with Orpheus playing until his fingers bled and still refusing to stop. Even Hymen, god of marriage, attended — though witnesses later said his torch flickered and smoked, an omen no one mentioned aloud. The couple noticed nothing but each other.

title: The Serpent in the Grass
description: While fleeing an unwanted pursuit, Eurydice steps on a serpent and is killed by its bite.
story: She had been running, laughing, calling back over her shoulder when her foot found the snake hidden in the long grass. The venom was fast and the meadow went quiet all at once. By the time Orpheus reached her, she was already cold.

title: Grief Without Music
description: Orpheus is consumed by grief so profound that the natural world mourns alongside him.
story: For forty days he did not touch the lyre. The trees shed their leaves out of season and the rivers ran silently, as if in respect. When he finally played again, the sound was so full of sorrow that the nymphs wept and the mountains cracked.

title: The Descent Begins
description: Orpheus makes the impossible decision to enter the Underworld and petition Hades for Eurydice's return.
story: He found the entrance at Taenarum, a cave that exhaled cold air like a sleeping giant. The shades at the threshold parted for him, drawn by the music that spilled unbidden from his fingers. He walked down into the dark, playing as he went.

title: Before the Throne of Hades
description: Orpheus plays for Hades and Persephone, and for the first time, the Underworld weeps.
story: The Furies covered their faces. Tantalus forgot his hunger and simply stood still. Persephone's cheeks were wet before the first verse was finished, and even Hades — stone-faced, immovable Hades — pressed his fist to his mouth and looked away. The petition was granted.

title: The Condition
description: Hades agrees to release Eurydice on one condition: Orpheus must not look back until they reach the living world.
story: The rule was simple and absolute. Eurydice would follow behind him through the dark. He would hear her footsteps — or think he did — the entire way. He was not to turn around until sunlight touched his face.

title: The Long Walk Up
description: Orpheus climbs toward the living world, Eurydice behind him, in an agony of love and doubt.
story: The silence between them was unbearable. He played to fill it, but the echoes in the tunnel were strange and he couldn't tell if her footsteps were real or imagined. With every step upward, his certainty dissolved a little more. The light ahead grew and so did his fear.

title: The Turn
description: At the threshold of the upper world, Orpheus turns to look — and loses Eurydice forever.
story: He told himself later that he saw a glimmer of light and panicked that she wasn't there. He told himself he acted out of love, not doubt. But the moment his eyes found her face, he watched it go pale and recede, her hand reaching briefly toward him before the dark took her back. She did not blame him. That was the worst part.

title: Music at the End of the World
description: Orpheus wanders the earth alone, his music transformed by a grief no living person can fully understand.
story: He played for anyone who would listen and for no one at all. The songs he made in those last years were so beautiful that wild animals lay down beside him and forgot to be hungry. When he finally died, the Muses gathered his scattered lyre and placed it among the stars, where it shines still.
